title: My first job at the rice shop
summary: At thirteen, the writer was given one job at a small rice shop: count the bags. The old owner had a quiet way of checking that the new boy could be trusted.
body:
When I was thirteen, I had my first job. I worked at a small rice shop. The shop was on Tran Phu Street.

The owner was Mr Linh. He was old. He had thick glasses. He could not see well. So he gave me one job: count the bags of rice. Every Monday, I counted. Every Friday, I counted again.

The first Monday, I counted forty-two bags. The first Friday, I counted forty. Two bags were missing.

I told Mr Linh. He smiled. He said, 'Are you sure?'

'I am sure,' I said. 'Two bags are missing.'

Mr Linh said, 'Good. The two bags are inside my house. I took them last night. I wanted to know if you would tell me.'

I was angry, but I did not show my face.

The next week, two bags were missing again. I told Mr Linh.

He smiled again. He said, 'Good. Now I know.'

I worked at the rice shop for one year. Mr Linh tested me many times. I always told him the true number.

When I left for high school, Mr Linh gave me a small book. Inside, he wrote: 'Some old men cannot see. But they listen.'

I still have the book.

grammarNotes.title: Negative past simple with 'did not'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Để nói câu phủ định ở quá khứ, dùng 'did not + động từ nguyên thể'. Trong bài: 'I did not show my face.' Lưu ý: với động từ 'be', dùng 'was not / were not' thay vì 'did not': 'He could not see well.' dùng 'could not' (modal phủ định).
grammarNotes.bodyEn: To make a past-simple negative, use 'did not + base verb'. From the story: 'I did not show my face.' Note: with 'be', use 'was not / were not' instead of 'did not'. With 'can', use 'could not': 'He could not see well.'

cloze.template:

The owner was Mr {{b3}}. He was old. He had thick {{b4}}. He could not see well. So he gave me one job: count the bags of rice. Every {{b5}}, I counted. Every Friday, I counted again.

The first Monday, I counted {{b6}} bags. The first Friday, I counted forty. Two bags were {{b7}}.

I told Mr Linh. He smiled. He said, 'Are you {{b8}}?'

'I am sure,' I said. 'Two bags are missing.'

Mr Linh said, 'Good. The two bags are inside my house. I took them last night. I wanted to know if you would tell me.'

I was {{b9}}, but I did not show my face.

The next week, two bags were missing again. I told Mr Linh.

He smiled again. He said, 'Good. Now I know.'

I worked at the rice shop for one year. Mr Linh {{b10}} me many times. I always told him the true number.

When I left for high {{b11}}, Mr Linh gave me a small {{b12}}. Inside, he wrote: 'Some old men cannot see. But they listen.'

blanks.explanation: A rice shop — where bags of rice are sold.
Mr Linh had thick glasses — and still could not see well.
He counted every Monday and every Friday.
'I counted forty-two bags' on the first Monday.
Two bags were missing — fewer than on Monday.
Mr Linh asked, 'Are you sure?' — testing the boy.
The boy was angry that Mr Linh had tested him — but did not show it.
Mr Linh tested the boy many times — to check his honesty.
When he left for high school — the next step in his life.
Mr Linh gave him a small book — with a sentence written inside.

translationVi:
Khi tôi mười ba tuổi, tôi có công việc đầu tiên. Tôi làm ở một cửa hàng gạo nhỏ. Cửa hàng nằm trên đường Trần Phú.

Chủ cửa hàng là ông Linh. Ông già rồi. Ông đeo kính dày. Ông nhìn không rõ. Vì vậy ông giao tôi một việc: đếm các bao gạo. Thứ Hai nào tôi cũng đếm. Thứ Sáu nào tôi cũng đếm lại.

Thứ Hai đầu tiên, tôi đếm bốn mươi hai bao. Thứ Sáu đầu tiên, tôi đếm bốn mươi. Thiếu hai bao.

Tôi nói với ông Linh. Ông cười. Ông hỏi: 'Cháu chắc chứ?'

'Cháu chắc,' tôi nói. 'Thiếu hai bao.'

Ông Linh nói: 'Tốt. Hai bao đó ở trong nhà ông. Tối qua ông đem về. Ông muốn biết cháu có nói cho ông không.'

Tôi giận, nhưng tôi không lộ ra mặt.

Tuần sau, lại thiếu hai bao. Tôi nói với ông Linh.

Ông lại cười. Ông nói: 'Tốt. Bây giờ ông biết rồi.'

Tôi làm ở cửa hàng gạo một năm. Ông Linh thử tôi nhiều lần. Tôi luôn nói cho ông con số thật.

Khi tôi đi học cấp ba, ông Linh tặng tôi một cuốn sách nhỏ. Bên trong, ông viết: 'Có những người già không thấy. Nhưng họ lắng nghe.'

Tôi vẫn còn giữ cuốn sách đó.

questions.prompt: How old was the writer when he had his first job?
questions.explanation: 'When I was thirteen, I had my first job.'

questions.prompt: What was the writer's job at the shop?
questions.explanation: 'He gave me one job: count the bags of rice.'

questions.prompt: Why did Mr Linh give him this job?
options: Mr Linh could not see well
questions.explanation: 'He could not see well. So he gave me one job.'

questions.prompt: How many bags did the boy count on the first Monday?
What happened to two of the bags by Friday?
options: They were missing — Mr Linh had taken them himself
questions.explanation: 'I took them last night. I wanted to know if you would tell me.'
questions.hint: Look at paragraph 6 — Mr Linh's reply.

questions.prompt: Why did Mr Linh do this?
options: To test whether the boy would tell him honestly
questions.explanation: 'I wanted to know if you would tell me.'

questions.prompt: How did the boy feel — and what did he show?
options: He felt angry, but did not show it on his face
questions.explanation: 'I was angry, but I did not show my face.'
questions.hint: Look at the short sentence after Mr Linh's reveal.

questions.prompt: How long did the boy work at the rice shop?
questions.explanation: 'I worked at the rice shop for one year.'
questions.hint: Look near the end of the story.

questions.prompt: What did Mr Linh give the boy when he left for high school?
options: A small book with a sentence written inside
questions.explanation: 'Mr Linh gave me a small book. Inside, he wrote: "Some old men cannot see. But they listen."'
questions.hint: Look at the last full paragraph.

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of the story?
options: Mr Linh was a bad person
An old man with weak eyes tested a young boy's honesty — and the boy kept being honest for a whole year
questions.explanation: Mr Linh tested him many times; the boy always told the true number.
questions.hint: Look at the pattern across the whole story.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Mr Linh wrote in the book: 'Some old men cannot see. But they listen.' Think of someone in your life whose attention you might have underestimated because of something they cannot do. What might they have noticed about you?
